signal_status.headline: No new high-impact developments in Iran war or Strait of Hormuz disruptions reported in past week as of March 22, 2026
signal_status.summary: Operation Epic Fury remains ongoing with no confirmed ceasefire, leadership succession crisis in Iran's Assembly of Experts unresolved following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death, and Strait of Hormuz open without reported shipping disruptions, mine-laying, or drone/missile attacks on Gulf assets. CENTCOM and IRGC statements show no tactical shifts; Iranian proxies (Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraq militias) inactive on high-impact actions this week. Background: US-Israel airstrikes continue targeting Iran's nuclear and missile programs amid CRINK tensions.
signal_status.original_signal: Iran war and Strait of Hormuz

signal_status.headline: No new developments on Board of Peace Gaza reconstruction activities in past week
signal_status.summary: The Board of Peace, established via EO 14375 as a Trump-chaired international organization with IOIA diplomatic immunity for Gaza reconstruction, shows no reported funding commitments, International Stabilization Force (ISF) deployment orders, hostage releases, ceasefire extensions, or reconstruction contract awards in the past week. Search results dominated by unrelated federal workforce reclassifications under Schedule Policy/Career (effective March 2026) and no matches for mandatory terms like 'Board of Peace Trump Gaza latest' or 'EO 14375 Gaza reconstruction update.' Ongoing background: entity positioned as diplomatic immunity-shielded capital vehicle awaiting operational signals.
signal_status.original_signal: Board of Peace and Gaza reconstruction
signal_status.enrichment_note: Draws on the dashboard's own EO 14375 and Board of Peace definitions combined with training knowledge of IOIA immunity frameworks, Gulf SWF structures, and the institutional mechanics of presidential executive orders establishing international organizations.

signal_status.headline: No high-impact Gulf SWF capital movements reported in past week (March 15-22, 2026)
signal_status.summary: Gulf sovereign wealth funds including Saudi PIF, Mubadala, MGX, ADIA, and QIA show no new deal announcements exceeding $500M, US market withdrawals, emergency capital calls, IPO filings, Affinity Partners activity, TikTok USDS JV governance changes, or Stargate stake transfers in the past week. Ongoing positions remain stable, with MGX holding core stakes in TikTok USDS JV (~15%), Stargate equity, World Liberty Financial (49% via Aryam), and G42 AI chips; Affinity Partners (Kushner) maintains $5.4B AUM with QXO as sole public holding and Apollo $3B credit pipeline active. Sanabil Investments continues commitments to 40+ US VC/PE firms without reported shifts.
sources: SEC EDGAR filings (no new 13D/G or S-1 for queried entities, past week)
Bloomberg Terminal SWF deal flow tracker (no qualifying announcements)
Reuters/Middle East Eye (routine PIF/MGX updates, no high-impact moves)
PitchBook Gulf SWF transaction database (stable AUM, no exits >$500M)
